other_observers: Joey Kellner, Kathy Mihm Dunning and some others.; I am the original finder
description: A cute little shorebird roughly the size of a Killdeer. He was mostly grey to grey-brown with fine pale edging to the coverts and mantle, no markings in the feather centers, buffy-whitish underneath with fine grey spots, sparse towards the rear and becoming more dense in the chest, white belly and undertail. A dark eyeline with a thin white supercilliam and brownish cap. A short, tubular looking bill reminding me of a Semipalmated Sandpiper's bill, but a bit thicker and a bit straighter. Head seemed small for the plump body. Legs were dark!Behaviors: Feeding on the shore of a lake as shorebirds do, and traveling along. The bird never flew during our observations.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
